_note: Upset radar — matches and groups with high chaos potential. Update as the tournament progresses.

upsets.title: South Africa shocks Mexico — at the Azteca
upsets.reasoning: Mexico are heavy favorites at their sacred Estadio Azteca on opening day. But South Africa are organized, physical, and play with nothing to lose. The Azteca crowd pressure could work against Mexico — and 'nothing to lose' upsets happen on opening days.
keyFactors: Opening match nerves affect favorites more than underdogs

upsets.title: Egypt ends USA's group stage dominance
upsets.reasoning: Egypt have one of the most dangerous strikers in African football and a deeply organized defensive structure. USA as co-host will carry expectation. If Egypt can neutralize the US midfield and get a set-piece goal, the crowd goes quiet quickly.
keyFactors: Egypt's African Cup of Nations form

upsets.title: Morocco tops Group C
upsets.reasoning: Morocco winning their group isn't exactly an upset — they're 2022 semifinalists. But they're often underseeded. If they come out of Group C at the top, it sets up a run.
keyFactors: Tournament experience at the highest level
upsets.upsetOdds: Moderate — not really an upset anymore

upsets.title: Chile beats Brazil in Group D
upsets.reasoning: Brazil are the pre-tournament favorites in Group D, but Chile have beaten Brazil in competitive South American football. A fiery CONMEBOL rivalry, both teams know each other well. If Brazil start slow and Chile score first, the pressure builds fast.
keyFactors: Chile's history vs Brazil in CONMEBOL

upsets.title: Indonesia — the group stage wildcard
upsets.reasoning: Indonesia are likely the longest shot in the tournament. But first World Cup appearances carry unpredictable energy. They play with pride and have nothing to lose. Their group opponents will need to be switched on — complacency is the danger.

upsets.title: Turkey knocks Argentina out of the group
upsets.reasoning: Argentina are defending champions and Group E favorites. But Turkey are a dangerous, tactically sophisticated side that has outperformed expectations in recent Euros. Group E could be tighter than expected. Messi at 38 against a young, physical Turkish midfield is an interesting matchup.

upsets.title: Scotland causes havoc in Group G
upsets.reasoning: Scotland's World Cup return after decades away will be electric. They are physical, organized, and dangerous on set pieces. Against Spain, who sometimes lack urgency in group stages, Scotland could capitalize.
keyFactors: Scotland's return to the World Cup
Spanish consistency variable in group stages
Player motivation on the world stage after long absence

upsets.title: Poland eliminates England
upsets.reasoning: England are heavy favorites in Group H. But they have a history of underperforming in group stages when the pressure isn't fully on. Poland with Lewandowski — even at 37 — can punish any England lapse. And England vs a European opponent is always nervous viewing.
keyFactors: Group H has a difficult balance of quality
England psychological fragility in group stages
